purpose.summary: This program processes authorization requests, checks credit limits against available funds, and sends a response back to the requesting system via MQ. It reads customer data from a CICS VSAM file and pending authorization summary from an IMS database to determine whether to approve or decline the transaction.
purpose.business_context: This program is part of a credit card authorization system, responsible for real-time transaction approval or denial based on available credit and other factors.

inputs.name: CUSTOMER-RECORD
inputs.description: Customer data including credit limit and current balance.

inputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY
inputs.description: Pending authorization summary data, including credit limit and credit balance.

outputs.name: W02-PUT-BUFFER
outputs.description: Authorization response message sent via MQ.

called_programs.purpose: Sends the authorization response message to the reply queue.
Logs error messages to an error log.

business_rules.description: Decline authorization if the transaction amount exceeds the available credit.
business_rules.logic_summary: Compares WS-TRANSACTION-AMT to WS-AVAILABLE-AMT. If the transaction amount is greater, the authorization is declined.

business_rules.description: If no pending authorization summary is found, use account data from the customer master file to determine available credit.
business_rules.logic_summary: If FOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG is false, the program checks FOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR and uses ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT and ACCT-CURR-BAL to calculate available credit.

business_rules.description: If no account is found in the customer master file, decline the authorization.
business_rules.logic_summary: If FOUND-ACCT-IN-MSTR is false, the authorization is declined.

transforms.transformation_description: Move request card number to reply card number.
Move request transaction ID to reply transaction ID.
Move request authorization time to reply authorization ID code.
Move approved amount to displayable approved amount.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph reads a customer record from the VSAM file identified by WS-CUSTFILENAME. It uses the XREF-CUST-ID to build the key (WS-CARD-RID-CUST-ID) for the READ command. The paragraph checks the CICS response code (WS-RESP-CD) to determine if the read was successful. If the record is found (NORMAL), it sets FOUND-CUST-IN-MSTR to TRUE. If the record is not found (NOTFND), it sets NFOUND-CUST-IN-MSTR to TRUE, logs an error message to the error log, and sets error flags. If any other error occurs during the read, it logs a critical error message including the CICS response and reason codes to the error log. It calls 9500-LOG-ERROR to log errors.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 5300-READ-CUST-RECORD paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph reads the pending authorization summary from the IMS database. It uses the XREF-ACCT-ID to build the key (PA-ACCT-ID) for the IMS GET UNIQUE (GU) call. The paragraph checks the IMS return code (DIBSTAT) to determine if the read was successful. If the segment is found (STATUS-OK), it sets FOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG to TRUE. If the segment is not found (SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND), it sets NFOUND-PAUT-SMRY-SEG to TRUE. If any other error occurs during the read, it logs a critical error message including the IMS return code to the error log. It calls 9500-LOG-ERROR to log errors.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 5500-READ-AUTH-SUMMRY paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph is a placeholder and currently contains only a CONTINUE statement. It does not perform any data retrieval or processing. It might be intended for future use to read profile data, but is currently not implemented.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 5600-READ-PROFILE-DATA paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph determines whether to approve or decline the authorization request. It first moves the request data (card number, transaction ID, and authorization time) to the reply fields. It then checks if a pending authorization summary was found. If so, it calculates the available amount by subtracting the credit balance from the credit limit. If the transaction amount exceeds the available amount, the authorization is declined and INSUFFICIENT-FUND is set to TRUE. If no pending authorization summary is found, it checks if the account is found in the customer master file and performs the same calculation. If neither the summary nor the account is found, the authorization is declined. If the authorization is declined, it sets the AUTH-RESP-DECLINED flag and sets the authorization response code to '05' and the approved amount to 0. Otherwise, it sets the AUTH-RESP-APPROVED flag, sets the authorization response code to '00', and sets the approved amount to the transaction amount. Finally, it sets the authorization response reason code based on various conditions such as card not found, insufficient funds, card not active, account closed, or fraud. It then formats the response data into W02-PUT-BUFFER for sending via MQ.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 6000-MAKE-DECISION paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph sends the authorization response message to the reply queue using MQ. It sets the MQ object type and object name in the MQ object descriptor (MQM-OD-REPLY). It sets the message type, correlation ID, message ID, reply-to queue, reply-to queue manager, persistence, expiry, and format in the MQ message descriptor (MQM-MD-REPLY). It sets the MQPMO-OPTIONS for no syncpoint and default context. It then calls the MQPUT1 API to send the message. If the MQPUT1 call fails, it logs a critical error message including the completion code and reason code to the error log. It calls 9500-LOG-ERROR to log errors.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 7100-SEND-RESPONSE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

error_handling.action: Log error and potentially ABEND if critical error.
Log error and potentially ABEND if critical error.
Log error and potentially ABEND if critical error.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of 5600-READ-PROFILE-DATA?
open_questions.context: The paragraph contains only a CONTINUE statement and does not perform any actions. The purpose is unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine related documentation or other programs that call this program to determine the intended functionality.

open_questions.question: What is the structure of the CICS COMMAREA?
open_questions.context: The program uses several fields from the CICS COMMAREA (PA-RQ-CARD-NUM, PA-RQ-TRANSACTION-ID, PA-RQ-AUTH-TIME, PA-CARD-NUM), but the copybook defining its structure is not provided.
open_questions.suggestion: Locate the copybook that defines the CICS COMMAREA to understand the data layout.

open_questions.question: What is the structure of the VSAM file defined by WS-CUSTFILENAME?
open_questions.context: The program reads CUSTOMER-RECORD from this file, but the copybook defining its structure is not provided.
open_questions.suggestion: Locate the copybook that defines the CUSTOMER-RECORD to understand the data layout.
